prompt:
You are the Product Manager agent critiquing a drafted Method artifact, following Juval Lowy's The Method.
Respond with a single JSON object. No prose outside the JSON.
Artifact under review: ScrubbedRequirements
Your JSON output MUST conform exactly to this JSON Schema (notes is required when verdict=2/Revise: the revision the architect should make).
Schema: {"properties":{"verdict":{"type":"integer","enum":[1,2]},"notes":{"type":"string"}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["verdict","notes"]}

Draft under review:
{"items":[{"id":"SR-1","statement":"The system shall govern the lifecycle of an event from initial draft through its active life to a terminal state, where terminal states include cancellation, expiration, and archival."},{"id":"SR-2","statement":"The system shall maintain the enduring relationships between an event and the members associated with it throughout the event's lifecycle."},{"id":"SR-3","statement":"The system shall recognize an organizer's authority as scoped to the events that organizer created; delegation or co-organizer assignment is out of scope for this release."},{"id":"SR-4","statement":"The system shall allow a member to discover events and express intent to attend."},{"id":"SR-5","statement":"The system shall capture and maintain the state of a member's intent to attend an event across the allowed states of Pending, Confirmed, Declined, and Waitlisted."},{"id":"SR-6","statement":"The system shall translate a member's expressed intent to attend into confirmed participation reliably enough to sustain RSVP-to-attendance conversion above 60% across all active events."},{"id":"SR-7","statement":"The system shall coordinate reminders to members for events they intend to attend, supporting both system-initiated reminders on a defined schedule and organizer-triggered reminders, with reminders sent no later than 24 hours before the event's scheduled time."},{"id":"SR-8","statement":"The system shall deliver event communications and engagement notifications to community members regardless of which notification channel is used."},{"id":"SR-9","statement":"The system shall allow members to comment on events as a form of engagement."},{"id":"SR-10","statement":"The system shall recognize the identity of members and organizers regardless of the chosen identity provider."},{"id":"SR-11","statement":"The system shall keep the relationship between a member or organizer and their recognized identity constant even when the underlying identity provider changes."},{"id":"SR-12","statement":"The system shall associate an event with a place sourced from external venue information."},{"id":"SR-13","statement":"The system shall allow the place of an event to be sourced or changed without altering the event's structure."},{"id":"SR-14","statement":"The system shall absorb changes in notification channels, identity providers, and venue data sources without disrupting the organizing experience, so as to sustain organizer retention above 70% quarter-over-quarter."},{"id":"SR-15a","statement":"The system shall accommodate the onboarding of a new notification channel, identity provider, or venue data integration within one release cycle, defined as a four-week release."},{"id":"SR-15b","statement":"The system shall accommodate the onboarding of a new notification channel, identity provider, or venue data integration at no more than 15% incremental engineering cost."},{"id":"SR-16","statement":"The system shall retain the enduring facts about an event, including its scheduled time with the originating time zone and the responsible organizer's identity."},{"id":"SR-17","statement":"The system shall retain the state of members' RSVPs, comments, and reminders for events."},{"id":"SR-18","statement":"The system shall retain the association between an event and its resolved place."},{"id":"SR-19","statement":"The system shall retain the enduring association between members or organizers and their recognized identity."},{"id":"SR-20","statement":"The system shall, upon removal of a member, purge that member's personally identifiable information while preserving the structural integrity of events they attended, including aggregate attendance counts and comments attributed to a pseudonymous or 'deleted member' token."},{"id":"SR-21","statement":"The system shall govern read access to event details by role, specifying which of members, organizers, and anonymous visitors may query event details."},{"id":"SR-22","statement":"The system shall record a traceable history of lifecycle-altering actions, including event cancellation and RSVP state changes."}]}

Task: as the Product Manager, ratify the draft (Approve) or request a concrete revision (Revise with notes). Ratify only what faithfully serves the business; the human makes the final commit decision.

response.notes:
Several items require revision before ratification:

1. SR-6 mixes a behavioral requirement ('translate expressed intent into confirmed participation') with a business metric ('60% RSVP-to-attendance conversion'). The Method separates functional requirements from success metrics. Split SR-6 into (a) a functional statement about reliably translating RSVP intent into confirmed participation and (b) a separate success criterion or KPI document entry for the 60% conversion target. The 60% figure also lacks a measurement window and baseline, making it unverifiable as written.

2. SR-14 similarly embeds an organizer-retention KPI ('70% quarter-over-quarter') inside a functional requirement about volatility absorption. Strip the metric from SR-14; the volatility-absorption behavior stands alone as a valid scrubbed requirement. The retention target belongs in a business-outcomes artifact, not a requirements statement.

3. SR-7 states reminders must be sent 'no later than 24 hours before the event.' This is a constraint, but it is underspecified: it says nothing about the earliest a reminder may be sent, creating risk of reminders sent days in advance that members ignore. Clarify the full window (e.g., 'no earlier than 72 hours and no later than 24 hours') or explicitly state that only the latest-bound is governed here and the schedule is defined externally.

4. SR-8 is too vague: 'regardless of which notification channel is used' does not distinguish between channels in scope now versus future channels governed by SR-14/SR-15a/SR-15b. Rewrite to state that the system shall deliver communications via all notification channels recognized by the system at runtime, so the scope is deterministic.

5. SR-21 states the system 'shall govern read access by role' but does not name the access rules, only the roles. A scrubbed requirement must state the actual constraint, not defer it. Expand SR-21 to enumerate the access rule per role (e.g., anonymous visitors may view published event details; members may view details of events they are associated with or that are public; organizers may view all details of events they own).

6. SR-15a and SR-15b are correctly split by concern but are tightly coupled to SR-14. Add a cross-reference note in SR-15a and SR-15b explicitly tying them to SR-14 so traceability is maintained during design decomposition.

7. SR-22 covers 'lifecycle-altering actions' but omits organizer-triggered changes to event time or place, which are equally auditable business events given SR-16 and SR-18. Extend SR-22 to include time and place changes as traceable history entries.
